title: Reconnect-safe LiveView with temporary_assigns and fresh handle_params
prompt: Write lib/my_app_web/live/live_dashboard_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.LiveDashboardLive). This dashboard shows: a large log buffer (stored in temporary_assigns: [logs: []] so it's emptied after each render and doesn't survive reconnects), a @now timestamp that must be fresh on every reconnect (so NOT via assign_new), and a @user_profile that must survive reconnect (so VIA assign_new from the session). mount/3 determines connection state and subscribes to "logs:stream" only on connected?/1. handle_info({:log, entry}, socket) appends to the @logs temporary assign. handle_params/3 reads current params and refreshes @now to DateTime.utc_now/0. The render function shows @logs, @now, and @user_profile.name. Key rule: assign_new for user_profile, plain assign for @now, temporary_assigns for @logs — three different assign strategies in one module.
tier_rationale: Requires knowing the semantic difference between assign_new (reuse across reconnects) and assign (fresh on each mount) and temporary_assigns (cleared after render). Sonnet typically picks one strategy for everything and doesn't understand when assign_new is unsafe (time-sensitive values) or when temporary_assigns is necessary (large buffers).
research_citation: oliver-kriska: 'assign_new silently skips on reconnect'; Phoenix docs: 'temporary_assigns clear after each render'
